mapping_policy: CMT mappings are downstream of MIPVU. Every non-exploratory mapping must cite metaphor-related or uncertain MIPVU lexical units from the same sentence.

mappings.expression: we must live through all time, or die by suicide
mappings.evidence_span: As a nation of freemen we must live through all time, or die by suicide.
mappings.conceptual_metaphor: THE NATION IS A LIVING BODY
entailments: A nation can live or die as though it were an organism.
National destruction can be framed as self-caused bodily death.
Political preservation becomes a duty of collective self-care.
mappings.rival_reading: Conventional body-politic idiom rather than active metaphor; keep conventionality visible in aggregate claims.
mappings.justification: The reviewed MIPVU units mark live, die, and suicide as personification/body-politic language in the same sentence.

mappings.expression: final resting place ... gave their lives that this nation might live
mappings.evidence_span: We have come to dedicate a portion of that field as a final resting place for those who here gave their lives that this nation might live.
mappings.conceptual_metaphor: SOLDIER DEATH IS A GIFT THAT PRESERVES NATIONAL LIFE
entailments: The soldiers' deaths are construed as meaningful offerings.
The nation is figured as a life that can be preserved by those deaths.
Burial and dedication convert loss into public obligation.
mappings.rival_reading: The sentence may be read as solemn memorial diction rather than a fully active sacrificial mapping.
mappings.justification: The mapping combines reviewed MIPVU evidence for resting, gave, and live in a single burial/dedication sentence.

mappings.expression: dedicated to the great task remaining before us
mappings.evidence_span: It is rather for us to be here dedicated to the great task remaining before us.
entailments: Political commitment is treated as solemn devotion.
The living inherit an obligation generated by the dead.
Public work is elevated above ordinary preference.
mappings.rival_reading: Dedicated may function as conventional ceremonial diction with limited conceptual force.
mappings.justification: The MIPVU unit dedicated records a contrast between public commitment and sacred/solemn setting apart.

mappings.expression: gave the last full measure of devotion
mappings.evidence_span: that cause for which they gave the last full measure of devotion
mappings.conceptual_metaphor: MILITARY DEATH IS TOTAL DEVOTIONAL PAYMENT
entailments: Death is framed as an offering or expenditure.
Devotion can be measured as though it were a quantity paid out.
The living can be obligated by the full payment of the dead.
mappings.rival_reading: Measure may be conventional quantifying language rather than a distinct accounting source domain.
mappings.justification: The mapping joins reviewed MIPVU evidence for gave and measure in the phrase that gives death an exchange/payment structure.

mappings.evidence_span: this nation, under God, shall have a new birth of freedom
entailments: Freedom can be renewed as a living thing comes into being.
War death becomes the condition for a future political life.
The nation is imagined as capable of rebirth rather than mere preservation.
mappings.rival_reading: Republican renewal formula; historical-semantics controls should prevent overstating biological literalness.
mappings.justification: Birth is a direct MIPVU metaphor in the reviewed sample and is explicitly linked to freedom and national future.

mappings.expression: government ... shall not perish from this earth
mappings.evidence_span: government of the people...by the people...for the people...shall not perish from this earth
mappings.conceptual_metaphor: DEMOCRATIC GOVERNMENT IS A LIVING ORGANISM
entailments: Government can die or survive as though it were alive.
The people's political form becomes a mortal life at risk.
Sacrificial duty is linked to democratic survival.
mappings.rival_reading: Perish can be a conventional verb for institutional failure; preserve conventionality in claims.
mappings.justification: The MIPVU unit perish is reviewed as personification in a climactic democratic-survival clause.

mappings.expression: all the wealth ... shall be sunk
mappings.evidence_span: until all the wealth piled by the bondsman's two hundred and fifty years of unrequited toil shall be sunk
entailments: Enslaved labor is treated as accumulated wealth with moral liability.
The wealth can be consumed or exhausted as part of judgment.
Economic gain is brought under providential accounting.
mappings.rival_reading: Sunk may be a financial or material idiom without a full metaphorical debt structure.
mappings.justification: The MIPVU unit sunk marks the movement from accumulated slave-produced wealth to exhaustion under divine will.

mappings.expression: every drop of blood ... shall be paid by another drawn with the sword
mappings.evidence_span: until every drop of blood drawn by the lash shall be paid by another drawn with the sword
entailments: Violence can be represented as payment for prior violence.
The Civil War becomes a moral accounting of slavery's lash.
Human blood is placed within a providential balance.
mappings.rival_reading: Sword remains uncertain: it may be literal weapon, metonym for war, or biblical idiom.
mappings.justification: The mapping links the high-confidence paid unit with the explicitly uncertain sword unit and preserves the uncertainty in the rival reading.

mappings.expression: The judgments of the Lord are true and righteous altogether
mappings.evidence_span: The judgments of the Lord are true and righteous altogether.
entailments: Providence is understood through legal judgment.
War suffering can be construed as sentence rather than accident.
Human claims are subordinated to divine adjudication.
mappings.rival_reading: Biblical quotation may function as doctrinal citation rather than independently mapped metaphor.
mappings.justification: The MIPVU unit judgments is uncertain, so this mapping is retained as provisional and low-confidence.
